entries.title: Critical MCP Integration Flaw Puts NGINX at Risk
entries.summary: Attackers can abuse the near-maximum severity flaw in nginx-ui to restart, create, modify, and delete NGINX configuration files.

entries.title: Navigating the Unique Security Risks of Asia's Digital Supply Chain
entries.summary: Regulatory differences, interconnected digital ecosystems, and the rise of AI have created a complex supply chain Asian organizations must wrangle.

entries.title: Prepping for 'Q-Day': Why Quantum Risk Management Should Start Now
entries.summary: Quantum computers are coming and may impact systems in unexpected ways, and it will "take years to be fully quantum-safe, if ever," cryptography expert warns.

entries.title: Audit: Big Tech Often Ignores CA Privacy Law Opt-Out Requests
entries.summary: Google, Meta, and Microsoft about half the time don't comply with requests to opt out of online tracking per a California law mandate, privacy watchdog finds.

entries.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
entries.summary: Two recently fixed prompt injections in Salesforce Agentforce and Microsoft Copilot would have enabled an external attacker to leak sensitive data.

entries.title: Microsoft Bets $10B to Boost Japan's AI, Cybersecurity
entries.summary: The deal aims to accelerate AI adoption, train workers, and develop cybersecurity partnerships — the latest move by a hyperscaler to compete for sovereign AI and data centers.

entries.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
entries.summary: Elevation-of-privilege bugs accounted for more than half of the 165 vulnerabilities patched, with two zero-days in that mix.

entries.title: EDR-Killer Ecosystem Expansion Requires Stronger BYOVD Defenses
entries.summary: Stopping EDR killers, which employ bring-your-own-vulnerable-driver (BYOVD) attack techniques, is difficult, but not impossible.

entries.title: War Game Exercise Demonstrates How Social Media Manipulation Works
entries.summary: In an educational game called "Capture the Narrative," students created bots to sway a fictional election, simulating influence in real-world political scenarios.

entries.title: Why Orgs Need to Test Networks to Withstand DDoS Attacks During Peak Loads
entries.summary: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax filing deadlines.

entries.title: CSA: CISOs Should Prepare for Post-Mythos Exploit Storm
entries.summary: In a new report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), experts warn of an "AI vulnerability storm" triggered by the introduction of Anthropic's Claude Mythos.

entries.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
entries.summary: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.

entries.title: Empty Attestations: OT Lacks the Tools for Cryptographic Readiness
entries.summary: OT asset owners are being asked by regulators to attest to their post-quantum cryptographic readiness without the appropriate tooling, resulting in paperwork dressed up to look like genuine security.

entries.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
entries.summary: The prolific China-backed threat group is targeting AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba cloud environments and using typosquatting to obscure C2 communication.

entries.title: Hims Breach Exposes the Most Sensitive Kinds of PHI
entries.summary: Threat actors breached the telehealth brand, and now they may know patients' personal health details. What could they do with that information?

entries.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
entries.summary: These are the fundamental detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to make to keep up with the rising number of credential-based attacks.

entries.title: FINRA Launches Financial Intelligence Fusion Center to Combat Cybersecurity and Fraud Threats
Orange Business Reimagines Enterprise Voice Communications With Trust and AI
Industrial Controllers Still Vulnerable As Conflicts Move to Cyber
entries.summary: The US government warns programmable logic controllers are being targeted, and research turns up 179 vulnerable operational technology (OT) devices.

entries.title: Can Anthropic Keep Its Exploit-Writing AI Out of the Wrong Hands?
entries.summary: Its Mythos Preview model, which can allegedly find and exploit critical zero-days, also comes with certain controls, the vendor said.

entries.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
entries.summary: Victims don't need to match the cyber espionage group's technical sophistication, experts say. But patching and some form of zero trust are now non-negotiable.

entries.title: 'BlueHammer' Windows Zero-Day Exploit Signals Microsoft Bug Disclosure Issues
entries.summary: Under the alias 'Chaotic Eclipse,' a researcher released a PoC exploit for a zero-day flaw that allows for system takeover by a local user, citing an undisclosed beef with Microsoft.

entries.title: Do Ceasefires Slow Cyberattacks? History Suggests Not
entries.summary: The cybersecurity community is waiting with bated breath to see if Iranian hackers will honor a ceasefire that doesn't actually name or directly involve them.

entries.title: Russia's Forest Blizzard Nabs Rafts of Logins via SOHO Routers
entries.summary: Heard of fileless malware? How about malwareless cyber espionage? Russia's APT28 is spying on global organizations by modifying just one DNS setting in vulnerable routers.

entries.title: Threat Actors Get Crafty With Emojis to Escape Detection
entries.summary: When 🤖 means "bot available," 🧰 signifies "toolkit," or 💰💰💰 translates to "big ransom," bad actors can evade filters and keep it all on the down-low.

entries.title: AI-Led Remediation Crisis Prompts HackerOne to Pause Bug Bounties
entries.summary: Discovery used to be the bottleneck for open source bugs, but with automated discovery, remediation's the bottleneck, which bounties don't fund.

entries.title: Fraud Rockets Higher in Mobile-First Latin America
entries.summary: Cyber-fraudsters move quickly from compromised devices to account takeover to funds transfer, shifting money before many financial institutions can react.

entries.title: Full Sail University to Open IBM Cyber Defense Range Powered by AWS and Cloud Range on Campus
Niobium Introduces The Fog
Pluralsight Launches SecureReady to Help Organizations Build Job-Ready Cybersecurity Teams
Iranian Threat Actors Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via Exposed PLCs
entries.summary: Attackers compromised Internet-facing OT devices and caused file and display manipulation, operational disruption, and financial losses across sectors.

entries.title: Storm-1175 Deploys Medusa Ransomware at 'High Velocity'
entries.summary: Microsoft says the financially motivated cybercrime group has exploited n-day and zero-day vulnerabilities in campaigns predicated on speed.

entries.title: Grafana Patches AI Bug That Could Have Leaked User Data
entries.summary: By hiding malicious instructions on an attacker-controlled Web page, AI could ingest orders that appear benign but return sensitive data to the attacker's server.

entries.title: RSAC 2026: How AI Is Reshaping Cybersecurity Faster Than Ever
entries.summary: Dark Reading's Kelly Jackson Higgins shares insights on the past, present, and future of cybersecurity after attending RSAC 2026 Conference.

entries.title: Human vs. AI: Debates Shape RSAC 2026 Cybersecurity Trends
entries.summary: As AI dominated RSAC 2026, CISOs and industry leaders debated its role in security, from agentic applications to the challenges of scaling human involvement in decision-making.

entries.title: Lies, Damned Lies, and Cybersecurity Metrics
entries.summary: A panel of five C-suite leaders discuss how cybersecurity success is measured and why it isn't improving results.

entries.title: Focusing on the People in Cybersecurity at RSAC 2026 Conference
entries.summary: AI dominated RSAC 2026 Conference, but it's still the humans in cybersecurity who matter most.

entries.title: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Attack Targets GitHub
entries.summary: PRT-scan is the second campaign in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration.

entries.title: Axios Attack Shows How Complex Social Engineering Is Industrialized
entries.summary: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.

entries.title: Fortinet Issues Emergency Patch for FortiClient Zero-Day
entries.summary: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild.

entries.title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
entries.summary: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.

entries.title: Shadow AI in Healthcare Is Here to Stay
entries.summary: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.

entries.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
entries.summary: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards group recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.

entries.title: Inconsistent Privacy Labels Don't Tell Users What They Are Getting
entries.summary: Data privacy labels are a great idea for mobile apps, but the current versions just aren't good enough.

entries.title: Apple Breaks Precedent, Patches DarkSword for iOS 18
entries.summary: Even organizations with users unwilling or unable to adopt iOS 26 can now protect themselves from a severe mobile OS-cracking tool.

entries.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
entries.summary: As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises.

entries.title: Picking Up 'Skull Vibrations'? Could Be XR Headset Authentication
entries.summary: "Skull vibration harmonics generated by vital signs" can be used to sign in to VR, AR, and MR headsets, according to emerging research.

entries.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
entries.summary: Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer.

entries.title: Chainguard Unveils Factory 2.0 to Automate Hardening the Software Supply Chain
entries.summary: The rebuilt Chainguard platform adds deeper security designed to continuously reconcile open source artifacts across containers, libraries, agent skills, and GitHub Actions.

entries.title: CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM Can Now Ingest Microsoft Defender Telemetry
entries.summary: Once CrowdStrike's nemesis, Microsoft is now a collaborator. A shared interest in Formula 1 helped thaw the years-long fierce rivalry.
